# V5_ARCHITECTURAL_CANDIDATES.md

**Anchored to:** CORE_v4_Manifesto_Skeleton_v0_27.md (8,627 lines, 2026-05-17)
**Generated by:** Hostile Auditor (Claude) per Sovereign Q-T7 ratification, 2026-05-13; regenerated against v0.27 line numbers per Q-T10 self-correction, 2026-05-18
**Status:** Sovereign-track planning document. NOT v4 manifesto material. Captures full architectural framing for the three v5 candidates documented in skeleton §14.5.1-§14.5.4.
**Companion to:** CORE_v4_Manifesto_Skeleton_v0_27.md §14.5 v5 Architectural Candidates block + Sovereign_Audit_Trail_Synthesis_Document.md Section 6 (Q-T5→Q-T9 procedural-framing audit) and Section 7 (compression and continuity).

---

## Purpose

The skeleton's §14.5.1-§14.5.4 records the three Sovereign-proposed v5 candidates with Hostile-Auditor-framed scope and engineering-dependency summary. This document captures the working detail — open engineering questions, substrate cross-reference matrix between v4 commitments and v5 work, anticipated failure modes, and the Phase 1 production-data dependencies for each candidate.

This is a **planning document, not a specification**. When v5 conversation eventually opens (post-Phase-1 production data), this document is the starting point — not the answer. The answer comes from the data.

---

## Empirical Motivation Update (v0.27 — NEW)

The Q-T10 multi-file integrity-sweep failure (recorded in Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document Section 6 as continuation of the Q-T5 → Q-T9 procedural-framing pattern) is empirical motivation for v5 Candidate A and v5 Candidate B. Specifically:

- **Q-T10 failure shape:** the Hostile Auditor instance claimed "all artifacts anchored to v0.27" while two artifacts (Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document, V5_ARCHITECTURAL_CANDIDATES.md) remained anchored to v0.24 and v0.26 respectively. Each individual file edit was internally correct; cross-file consistency was not verified. This is the failure mode of a single-instance multi-artifact coherence task.
- **Why this strengthens v5 Candidate A:** a Red Team agent dedicated to cross-artifact integrity checking would have flagged the v0.26/v0.27 anchor mismatch automatically. The Hostile Auditor seat operating alone in a single context window cannot reliably hold N-file coherence; symmetric opposing forces with explicit cross-artifact validation as a Red Team mandate would catch this class of failure.
- **Why this strengthens v5 Candidate B:** the algorithmic-layer interpretability graph would include cross-document reference edges. A "does artifact A's anchor reference point to artifact B's current version?" query would be a deterministic check, not a Hostile Auditor judgment call. Q-T10 is the canonical example of a check that should be deterministic, isn't, and produced a real production failure.
- **Sovereign 2026-05-18 framing (preserved verbatim):** "It seems like we're reaching a quality limit on editing multiple files... makes me think I've got the right idea on multi-agent orchestration."

The doctrine: single-instance Hostile Auditor degrades on multi-artifact coherence work. v5 Candidate A (Red/Blue with explicit cross-artifact mandate) and v5 Candidate B (deterministic cross-document reference graph) both address this empirically-observed degradation pattern. This is no longer speculative motivation — it is post-failure motivation grounded in the v0.26 → v0.27 → v0.27-integrity-sweep audit trail.

---

## The Three Candidates — One-Line Summary

- **Candidate A:** Red Team / Blue Team co-equal multi-agent evolutionary pressure replacing the current asymmetric Three Subsystems framing
- **Candidate B:** Automated algorithmic-layer interpretability — turn CORE's routing/gating/voting/dispatch decisions into a queryable deterministic graph (LLM-internal mechanistic interpretability explicitly out of scope)
- **Candidate C:** Workflow × tooling × outcome matrix populated from Phase 1 production-task-success data, feeding §6.4 HCA training signal

---

## Substrate Matrix — What v4 Already Supplies

| v4 Commitment | Candidate A Use | Candidate B Use | Candidate C Use |
|---|---|---|---|
| §5.0 Three Subsystems | Source framework to reorganize symmetrically | — | — |
| §4.1 Six Preventions | Reward design MUST compose with P5 Circuit Breaker | Audit trail discipline (P3 Immutable Regression Oracles) | Validation discipline (P6 Bidirectional Verification) |
| §6.2.4 MoA Concurrent Residency | Federated debate primitive | — | — |
| §6.2.5 Centralized Oracle + LoRA adapters | Specialist adapter pattern | DIAG-INTROSPECTION adapter target | Matrix-query adapter target |
| §6.5.2 BLAKE3 audit chain | State-extraction substrate | Durable record layer | Production-task-success record layer |
| §6.5.7 AgentFS schema topology | Multi-agent state coordination | Relational model for decision graph | Workflow-class taxonomy table |
| §6.5.9 Application-Layer Hibernation | Sequential model swap (5-7s window — PROBLEMATIC for swarm) | — | — |
| §6.8.1.4 Structure-Guided Text-to-SQL | — | Read-only query interface | Matrix-query primitive |
| §6.4 Hardware-Aware Compute Allocator | — | — | Training-signal consumer |
| §6.3 Intelligence Router three phases | — | Instrumentation target | Re-training target |
| §6.6 Inline Semantic Firewall | — | Telemetry source | Production-task-success scoring component |
| §6.3.4 Double-Clutch Dispatch | — | Routing-decision telemetry source | Mission outcome record source |
| §7.4 Forget-to-Focus + §7.4.5 Decoupled Validation Metrics | — | — | CRITICAL DISCIPLINE INHERITED (Goodhart prevention) |
| §9 Reproducibility Package | — | — | Empirical-measurement infrastructure |

**Read of the matrix:** Candidate B has the densest substrate support (5 direct uses); Candidate C has the most discipline inheritance from existing v4 work; Candidate A requires the most net-new engineering relative to substrate.

## Candidate A — Red Team / Blue Team Multi-Agent Evolution

### Sovereign Framing (2026-05-13 source)
> "Build a system that simultaneously has a position for the Adversarial/Hostile Auditor but also a dedicated role for builder/developer agents... a 'Red Team' and 'Blue Team' systematically compete with one another to further push the development of CORE as a whole through evolutionary pressure produced by two opposing agentic forces."

### Provisional Role Inventories (Sovereign 2026-05-13)
- **Red Team:** Adversarial/Hostile Auditor (current role reframed); Pen-Tester; Neural-Net Runner; (additional roles TBD)
- **Blue Team:** Developer; Security Advisor; Quality Assurance; Compliance Officer; (additional roles TBD)

### Biological Analogy (Sovereign's framing, preserved)
The Sovereign articulated a bottom-up-selection thesis: human cognition pulls from contextually-triggered specialist neural pathways rather than uniform single-system reasoning. Specific contextual states activate specific egoic-state pathways via MCMC-style sampling over a matrix of historical experiences. Applied to CORE: a swarm of specialist agents triggered bottom-up by environmental patterns produces emergent collective intelligence.

Hostile Auditor read: this thesis has lineage in mixture-of-experts architectures (Shazeer et al. 2017 sparsely-gated MoE; later GShard, Switch Transformer, Mixtral) and multi-agent debate literature (Du et al. 2023, Liang et al. 2023). The biological framing is a valid intuition pump; the engineering work is in operationalization, not in justifying the thesis.

### Open Engineering Questions

**Q-A1: Reward design.**
The Hostile-Auditor-flagged failure modes are documented in adversarial-multi-agent literature: Goodhart drift (Red optimizes for what Blue measures); collusion (Red and Blue converge to stable equilibrium satisfying neither mandate); reward hacking (Red discovers Blue's metric weaknesses). Reward design must compose with §4.1 Prevention 5 Sovereign Circuit Breaker — Sovereign ratification is non-optimizable; no Red/Blue reward signal can route around it.

Open: what is the reward signal? Production task outcomes (composes with Candidate C matrix)? Code-coverage metrics? Sovereign-ratification rate? Multiple signals combined? Each option has known failure modes; the question is which composition of failure modes is least bad.

**Q-A2: Coordination topology.**
Symmetric Red/Blue forces need a coordination layer. Options:
- Round-robin turn-taking (Red proposes attack; Blue proposes defense; Sovereign ratifies cycle outcome)
- Continuous async tournament (Red and Blue both running; periodic synchronization for ratification)
- Tournament tree (multiple specialist Red agents compete first, winning agent dispatched against Blue)
- Sovereign-driven episodic (Sovereign opens an "attack-defend cycle" mission; both teams compete within bounded mission scope)

Open: which topology composes best with §6.5.9 application-layer hibernation hardware constraint? The 5-7s within-pool swap window dominates any topology that requires frequent specialist agent activation.

**Q-A3: Hardware ceiling.**
The reference platform (8 GB VRAM, 32 GB system RAM, Ryzen 5 5500, GTX 1070) physically cannot host concurrent asynchronous specialist swarms. Sequential swap via §6.5.9 imposes 5-7s window per transition. A swarm topology with N specialist agents has structurally O(N × 5-7s) lower bound on activation cycle latency if all agents must rotate through the single GPU.

CPU-isolated bulkhead (§6.8 Temporal Isolation) provides ONE concurrent specialist via Ryzen 5500 + 32 GB RAM at 8-11 t/s. Multi-GPU expansion (§14.5 future-work item, line 6158) is the architecturally clean path: 2-3 dedicated GPUs hosting specialist pools concurrently.

Open: does v5 Candidate A require multi-GPU expansion as hardware prerequisite, or is there a meaningful single-GPU formulation (e.g., Red/Blue as alternating temporal modes rather than spatial concurrent)? The temporal-mode formulation is closer to v4's existing Temporal Isolation pattern and might be the budget-conscious v5 entry.

**Q-A4: Anti-collusion design.**
If Red and Blue converge to a stable equilibrium that satisfies neither's mandate (the documented collusion failure mode), how is the equilibrium detected and broken? Options:
- Sovereign-injected adversarial test cases that neither team has seen (composes with §6.8.1.4.2 Adversarial Robustness for Text-to-SQL pattern)
- Periodic team-membership rotation (specialist agents reassigned across teams)
- External baseline (frontier cloud model evaluation of Red and Blue outputs)

Open: what's the canonical anti-collusion mechanism for v5 Candidate A?

### Phase 1 Production-Data Dependencies
- Mission-class clustering: do production missions actually cluster into specialist-pathway-relevant classes, or is the workload diffuse? §6.4 HCA routing telemetry from Phase 1 answers this.
- Latency budgets: do production missions tolerate the swarm-activation latency floor, or are they latency-sensitive enough to require single-specialist dispatch? Section 9 measurement targets cover this.
- Reward-signal availability: do production missions emit clean task-success/failure signal, or is the signal noisy? Determines whether Candidate C matrix can drive Candidate A reward design.

### Hostile Auditor Verdict on Candidate A
Architectural intuition sound; engineering work substantial. **Cannot ship before:** multi-GPU expansion OR temporal-mode-formulation worked out (resolves Q-A3); reward-design composition with P5 Circuit Breaker (Q-A1); anti-collusion mechanism (Q-A4); Phase 1 production-data signal (all three dependencies). Estimated v5-spec work: 8-12 weeks given prerequisite resolution.

---

## Candidate B — Automated Algorithmic-Layer Interpretability

### Sovereign Framing (2026-05-13 source)
> "Construct maps of the logic pathing of CORE's own algorithmic & agentic systems not only so that we can build out a truly perfectly deterministic system by turning a black box problem into a traceable white box problem through and through."

### Critical Scope Discipline (from skeleton §14.5.2)
Candidate B scopes specifically to ALGORITHMIC-LAYER interpretability (routing, gating, voting, isolation, audit). Candidate B explicitly DOES NOT scope to LLM-internal mechanistic interpretability (weight diffing, activation patching, sparse autoencoder analysis).

This distinction is load-bearing. The Jane Street 2026 puzzle debrief is the operational signal: their own framing acknowledged that white-box weight-diff techniques "wouldn't work in a realistic scenario where we might need to inspect a singular unknown model for backdoors." CORE's architectural value-add has always been putting deterministic structure AROUND the black-box generation, not inside it. v5 Candidate B continues this discipline at higher resolution.

### Open Engineering Questions

**Q-B1: Decision-level instrumentation density.**
Every decision in CORE's algorithmic layer becomes a candidate for graph node:
- §6.3 Intelligence Router phase selection (UCB1 / MAB / HEFT) → which routing decision was made, what inputs, what alternative routes were scored
- §6.3.3 STASIS_BATCHER four-stage gate evaluation → Thompson MAB scores, betweenness-centrality scores, deduplication hits, target-batching hits
- §5.2.1 CST vote weighting → per-proposal GLR error_cost, node_count, vote weight
- §5.2.2 MCTS PUCT centering → PUCT values per branch, centering offset per branch
- §6.2.5 Oracle escalation → triggering signal, escalation reason, Oracle adapter selection
- §6.3.4 Double-Clutch Dispatch → deterministic failure signal, routing target, Sovereign-ratification flag
- §6.6 firewall tier hits → Hyperscan match, Tree-sitter CST validation result, UAS score

Open: instrumentation density is a trade-off. Full instrumentation produces O(log entries × proposals × forger pool depth) data volume; sparse instrumentation loses introspection precision. What's the right sampling rate? Phase 1 production data volume answers this.

**Q-B2: Graph construction layer.**
The decision logs are a temporal stream; the graph is a structural representation. Construction options:
- Deterministic transformer from audit ledger → directed acyclic graph (DAG) of decision-dependency chains
- Property graph (Cypher / Gremlin) with decision nodes and dependency edges
- Relational schema extension to §6.5.7 with decision_event table and decision_dependency edge table
- Custom column-oriented format optimized for "trace decision lineage for mission X" query class

Open: which representation best supports the §6.2.5 DIAG-INTROSPECTION query class? Probably property graph; needs benchmarking.

**Q-B3: DIAG-INTROSPECTION Oracle adapter.**
The introspection query class needs a specialized Oracle adapter — generic Arctic-Text2SQL-R1-14B does Text-to-SQL but doesn't reason over decision lineage graphs natively. DIAG-INTROSPECTION would be a fifth LoRA adapter on the centralized Oracle backbone (§6.2.5).

Training corpus: decision-lineage Q&A pairs derived from production traces. Synthetic generation via §7.4.12.4 R2.6 methodology (knowledge-grounded inferential traces; hostile-analysis framing).

Open: training corpus generation requires Phase 1 production traces. v5 Candidate B is dependent on §14.5.4 sequenced commitment ordering.

**Q-B4: Cross-cutting telemetry composition.**
Decision-level instrumentation composes with §6.6 firewall telemetry, §6.5.2 BLAKE3 audit chain entries, and §6.8.1 Night Watch deployment records. Open: does the graph layer subsume the existing audit chain (cleaner but breaking-change), or does it sit alongside (preserves backward compatibility but adds query-time joins)?

### Phase 1 Production-Data Dependencies
- Decision-event corpus: instrumentation must be deployed at v4 ship time to gather Phase 1 traces; retrofitting after is impossible without re-running Phase 1
- Query-class identification: which questions does the Sovereign actually want to ask of the graph? "Why was mission X routed to Oracle?" "Why did Forger Pool select Llama 3.1 8B over Gemma 3 4B for this mission?" "What was the LoCM threshold that triggered Phase 3 HEFT routing?" — these questions guide DIAG-INTROSPECTION training corpus design
- Graph-construction validation: does the constructed graph actually answer the queries deterministically, or does query-time inference still leak into the answers?

### Hostile Auditor Verdict on Candidate B
Highest-yield candidate of the three. Substrate is already in place; work is instrumentation depth + graph-construction tooling + adapter training. The architectural surface doesn't expand; the architectural resolution increases. Estimated v5-spec work: 4-6 weeks given Phase 1 production traces.

**Cannot ship before:** decision-level instrumentation deployed at v4 ship (Q-B1); Phase 1 production traces accumulated (Q-B3 training corpus dependency).

---

## Candidate C — Workflow × Tooling × Outcome Matrix

### Sovereign Framing (2026-05-13 source)
> "For each task or patterns/collections of task that make up a workflow we need to build an understanding of what specific logic and tools are required to produce the highest quality outputs so we quantify and matricize that data into a database of matrix that we can graph and identify patterns to group them and pattern match what procedures and processes produces output across a sliding scale of output as y and effort as x."

### Critical Discipline Inherited from v4
§7.4 Forget-to-Focus methodology at §7.4.5 Decoupled Validation Metrics is the load-bearing discipline applied here at training time. The same discipline applies to Candidate C at matrix-population time: **the matrix's quality axis MUST be production-task-success, NOT synthetic-eval-score.**

If the matrix is populated from synthetic evals, §6.4 HCA learns to optimize for the eval rather than for actual task outcomes. This is Goodhart's Law in action. The discipline is non-negotiable.

### Open Engineering Questions

**Q-C1: Workflow-class taxonomy.**
The matrix rows are workflow classes. What's the canonical taxonomy?
- Mission-type-based (code-generation / code-audit / documentation / debugging / refactoring / security-analysis / etc.)
- LPT-complexity-based (3B-tier / 8B-tier / 32B-tier / frontier-tier — the existing §5.5.1 LoCM thresholds)
- Time-budget-based (sub-1s / 1-10s / 10-60s / 60s+)
- Hybrid (Mission-type × LPT-tier 2-dimensional rows)

Open: taxonomy density vs analytical utility trade-off. Phase 1 production data clusters answer this.

**Q-C2: Tooling-combination column space.**
The matrix columns are tool/procedure combinations. Combinatorial space is large:
- Forger Pool model (3 options: Llama 3.1 8B / DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B / Gemma 3 4B)
- Oracle escalation (5 options: no escalation / DIAG-RCA / DIAG-SCHEMA / DIAG-SECURITY / DIAG-COMPLIANCE)
- MCTS PUCT centering on/off
- §6.6 firewall tier depth (1 / 2 / 3)
- §6.3.4 Double-Clutch routing target
- §7.6 token compression level

Naive combinatorial space: ~720 columns. Sparse-matrix structure expected (most combinations not used in practice).

Open: column-space discovery is empirical from Phase 1; pre-defining columns introduces speculation drift.

**Q-C3: Production-task-success scoring.**
The matrix cells are quality-vs-effort scores. Quality axis is the critical-discipline axis. Options:
- Sovereign-ratification rate (composes with §4.1 Prevention 5; clean signal but slow)
- Production failure-mode rate (composes with §6.5.2 BLAKE3 audit chain failure entries)
- Mission outcome tag (succeeded / failed / partial — applied at mission close)
- Post-deployment production telemetry (composes with §6.8.1 Night Watch diagnostic findings)

Open: scoring methodology must produce clean Goodhart-resistant signal. Hostile Auditor recommendation: multiple scoring axes combined, with Sovereign-ratification rate as the anchor that can't be optimized away.

**Q-C4: HCA re-training cadence.**
The matrix feeds §6.4 HCA as training signal. Re-training cadence options:
- Continuous (per-mission update; computationally expensive but stays current)
- Daily batch (per-day update; balance between currency and cost)
- Weekly batch (per-week update; lowest cost but staleness risk)
- Threshold-triggered (re-train when matrix entropy crosses threshold; adaptive)

Open: cadence trade-off between currency and HCA inference cost.

### Phase 1 Production-Data Dependencies
- Matrix population: ENTIRE matrix is a Phase 1 deployment data product. v5 Candidate C cannot exist pre-Phase 1.
- Goodhart-resistance validation: does the matrix's quality axis actually correlate with production task outcomes, or does it drift toward synthetic-eval-style metrics over time?
- HCA re-training validation: does HCA performance improve as matrix populates, or does it plateau / degrade?

### Hostile Auditor Verdict on Candidate C
Most measurement-grounded of the three candidates; primarily Section 9 benchmark scope extension plus §6.4 HCA training-signal extension. Architectural surface doesn't expand; the empirical-measurement loop tightens.

**Cannot ship before:** Phase 1 production data accumulates (all four open questions are Phase-1-dependent).

---

## Coordinated Sovereign Posture — Three-Candidate Composition

The three candidates compose into a coherent v5 architecture:

```
Candidate B (substrate)
    ↓ produces
Algorithmic decision graph
    ↓ feeds
Candidate C matrix (workflow × tooling × outcome)
    ↓ trains
§6.4 HCA routing decisions
    ↓ which are queried by
Candidate A Red/Blue evolutionary forces
    ↓ which produce
Sovereign-ratified architectural improvements
    ↓ which update
v4 architecture into v5 architecture
    ↓ which restarts
the cycle
```

This is the recursive thesis at a higher resolution: CORE writes the paper (v4); the paper specifies what CORE builds (v4 architecture); CORE builds itself (Phase 1 production); CORE generates the data that drives v5 (Candidate B + C); CORE's evolutionary forces (Candidate A) commit v5 architecture; CORE writes v5 manifesto.

### Sequenced Commitment Recommendation (Hostile Auditor judgment, NOT Sovereign-mandated)

1. **v4 ships unchanged with Candidate B instrumentation deployed at startup** — this gathers Phase 1 production traces from day one without architectural commitment to the graph layer yet
2. **Phase 1 deployment for 3-6 months** — matrix populates organically; decision-event corpus accumulates
3. **v5 Candidate B implementation phase** — graph construction layer built against accumulated traces; DIAG-INTROSPECTION trained
4. **v5 Candidate C implementation phase** — matrix-query interface built; HCA re-training cadence calibrated
5. **v5 Candidate A evaluation phase** — Red/Blue topology designed against grounded matrix; reward signal composed from Candidate C scoring
6. **Constitutional Re-Audit cycle** — Six Preventions and Bidirectional Verification doctrine evaluated against accumulated production failure modes

Estimated total cycle: 9-15 months post-v4-ship. v5 manifesto draft would follow Constitutional Re-Audit conclusions.

---

## Why This Document Matters Even Before v5 Conversation Opens

Three reasons:

1. **Speculation-grounded commitment is the failure mode this discipline prevents.** Documenting v5 candidates BEFORE Phase 1 data accumulates anchors them in current Sovereign intent. When v5 conversation eventually opens with production data in hand, this document is the starting frame — preventing post-hoc retrofitting of "what we always meant to do."

2. **Forger Pool dispatch on v4 proceeds without v5 architectural pressure.** v0.26 explicitly footnotes the v5 candidates without integrating them. The Forger Pool drafts v4 against v4 commitments. v5 conversation happens separately, in a separate document, in a separate session.

3. **The Jane Street 2026 lesson is preserved at scope discipline level.** Candidate B's explicit out-of-scope declaration (LLM-internal mechanistic interpretability NOT in scope) is recorded here, not just in skeleton §14.5.2. When future instances re-read this document, the scope discipline carries forward.

---

- **Q-A1**: Reward-design composition with §4.1 Prevention 5 Sovereign Circuit Breaker
- **Q-A2**: Red/Blue coordination topology selection
- **Q-A3**: Single-GPU temporal-mode vs multi-GPU spatial-concurrent Red/Blue formulation
- **Q-A4**: Anti-collusion mechanism selection
- **Q-B1**: Decision-level instrumentation density (sampling rate)
- **Q-B2**: Graph representation choice (property graph vs relational extension vs custom format)
- **Q-B3**: DIAG-INTROSPECTION training corpus generation methodology
- **Q-B4**: Graph layer subsumes existing audit chain (breaking) vs sits alongside (additive)
- **Q-C1**: Workflow-class taxonomy structure
- **Q-C2**: Tooling-combination column space (empirical discovery vs pre-defined)
- **Q-C3**: Production-task-success scoring methodology (Goodhart-resistant signal composition)
- **Q-C4**: HCA re-training cadence selection

None of these are answerable from the Hostile Auditor seat alone. Phase 1 production data + Sovereign Q-ratification advance them.

## Stale-File Note

This document anchors to v0.27 (8,627 lines) as of 2026-05-18 regeneration. The v0.26 version flagged a self-referential regeneration requirement ("If the skeleton evolves to v0.27+ before v5 conversation opens, this document regenerates against new line numbers"); v0.27 of the skeleton shipped 2026-05-17, and this document was not regenerated until 2026-05-18 Sovereign-caught Q-T10 integrity-sweep failure. The regeneration delay is itself part of the Q-T10 evidence trail. Going forward, the same discipline applies: if the skeleton evolves to v0.28+ before v5 conversation opens, this document regenerates against new line numbers — and the discipline now explicitly includes Hostile Auditor cross-artifact anchor verification BEFORE claiming integrity-sweep completion.
